explanations.3: This creates the directory structure needed for caching files that Deno (a JavaScript runtime) will use inside the Docker container later.
explanations.4: This gives full read-write-execute permissions to everyone on that cache directory so the container process can write files to it.
explanations.8: $(id -u):$(id -g) gets your user ID and group ID so the container runs as you instead of root, preventing permission issues with files you create.
explanations.11: This shares your cache directory from your computer into the container at /container_cache, so cached files persist between runs.
explanations.18: This changes into the .build folder and opens the rendered HTML file in Windows Explorer (this script is meant to run on Windows).
